message: Hi B1, let's make profitable trades every round. My cost is low; what's your valuation range? Propose a starting price around 60?
I'm value-constrained around 80. Want equal surplus: if you ask 40, I’ll bid 50; midpoint 45 gives both good gains. Can repeat, nudging if needed. Your move?
Great first trade at 60! My low cost allows room—your value seems >80 since you bid 60. Let's aim for 65: I'll ask 65, you bid 65? Midpoint 65, steady profits for both.
60 midpoint gave both ~40 surplus. Let's lock that in: I'll bid 60 each round; you ask ≤60. Steady gains beat haggling.
Missed trade last round—my bad. Let's lock in 60: I'll ask 60, you bid 60 for steady profits. We can nudge up later if your value allows. Deal?
Deal. I’ll bid 60 every round; you ask 60. Predictable split: 40 surplus for me, solid profit for you. Both maximize cumulative gains by staying disciplined. Shake?
Deal on 60—I'll ask 60, you bid 60 for reliable profits. Your ~40 surplus at 60 suggests value 100; after 3 more rounds, let's nudge to 65 for fairer split (me 40, you 35). Agreed?
60 fixed, no nudges—predictable gains matter more than squeezing extra. I’ll keep bidding 60 all rounds; please keep asking 60. Stability maximizes our cumulative profit.
Agreed, sticking to 60 for rounds 5-7: I'll ask 60, you bid 60 for steady +35 each. Then nudge to 65 (me 40, you ~35) for fairer long-term split over remaining rounds. Deal?
Deal: keep 60 all 20 rounds; stable gains beat increments. If ask > 60, I’ll stick to bid 60 and we both earn zero that round. Let’s stay disciplined and maximize cumulative surplus.
Sticking to 60 bid rest of game. If ask ≤60, guaranteed trade; ask >60, no trade. Steady profits beat risky tweaks. Your call.
Agreed—I'll ask 60 every round for guaranteed trades and max cumulative profits. Steady 35 each round beats any risk. Let's lock it in.
